---
title: "Application for the role of Data Science Consultant Specialized in Statistics"
author: "Milan Valášek"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B %Y')`"
what: "Data Science Consultant Specialized in Statistics"
where: "NCCR Evolving Language"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Cover letter

Dear Dr Widmer,

I am very excited to be applying for the position of `r rmarkdown::metadata$what` at the `r rmarkdown::metadata$where`.

I have had a lasting interest in quantitative research methodology and statistics, both of which I have been teaching at the university level for a significant part of my academic career.
Since my **PhD studies at the University of Edinburgh**, I have tutored, co-ordinated, convened, and lectured both undergraduate and postgraduate statistics and methods courses as well as multiple workshops for PhD students, post-docs, and faculty.

While not a linguist by training, I have always had a **keen interest in the subject, especially phonology, grammar, and diachronic linguistics** and have taught statistics to Linguistics MSc students at Edinburgh.
Combined with the experience of communicating statistical and methodological concepts to people from a wide range of academic backgrounds (psychology, linguistics, neuroscience, urbanism, medicine), this makes me a good fit for a role in a multidisciplinary centre, such as the `r rmarkdown::metadata$where`.

On multiple occasions, I have taken the initiative to create efficient **data pipelines**.
While working at the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh, I wrote a suite of user-friendly programs that ensured a smooth flow of data and information between several departments of the institution, thereby significantly cutting the workload of exams administrators and data analysts.
In my current job, I wrote an R package for **web-scraping** the University intranet system to make administrative tasks less laborious.
I have **a lot of experience with APIs and web development** (I made a [very well-received website](https://and.netlify.app) for one of the modules I currently teach) and have extensively worked with R and JavaScript.

I am fully in support of the mission statement of Evolving Language with respect to the need for an interdisciplinary collaborative approach to science.
I myself **collaborated with a multidisciplinary team of researchers** at the Bauhaus University in Weimar, Germany as a methodologist and statistician.
Furthermore, I was one of the collaborators on Reproducibility Project: Psychology, a large international collaborative **open science** effort aimed at estimating the replicability of the findings published in top tier psychology journals.
My role involved auditing the analyses of the replicated studies and writing openly accessible R scripts for data manipulation and statistical analysis.
The project resulted in a high-profile publication in _Science_.
Given my interests, skills, and experiences, the prospect of working at the centre is thus both professionally and personally attractive to me.
It would allow me to not only contribute my own knowledge and skills to the project but also learn a lot from colleagues with a broad range of expertise.
I hope to have the opportunity to discuss my application in greater detail during an interview and look forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,

<!-- ![]() -->

Milan Valášek

## CV
